title: Vegetable Bin Cupboard
summary: A three-shelf pantry cupboard with a face frame and three chicken-wire-front doors, keeping produce visible and ventilated instead of sealed behind solid panels.
description:
The chicken wire is what makes this a produce cupboard instead of a generic cabinet — solid doors trap humidity and hide what's going soft, while open wire fronts let air circulate and let you see at a glance what needs eating. Staple the wire to the inside of each door frame before hanging it; it's much easier to pull taut against a flat frame on the bench than after the door is already on its hinges.

Build the face frame as its own flat assembly before attaching it to the carcass, then cut each door to fit its own opening — 1/4" smaller in both height and width — rather than trusting a single fixed door size across all three, since even careful casework construction leaves openings a hair different from each other.

tools.note: Attaching the chicken wire to the door frames.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, beadboard, or other backing, 1/4 sheet
Chicken wire or wire mesh and staples

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood, beadboard, or other backing, 1/4 sheet

cutList.note: 2 per door; cut to fit each opening, 1/4" less than actual height.
2 per door; cut to fit each opening, 1/4" less than actual width.

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Build the basic carcass out of the 1x12 boards, using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, then attach the completed face frame to the front of the cupboard with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood back with 1" finish nails and glue.
materials: 1/4" plywood, beadboard, or other backing, 1/4 sheet

steps.title: Add the base moulding
steps.body: Cut the base moulding to fit, mitering the front corners. Nail on with nails long enough to pass through the moulding into the 3/4"-thick sides and front (typically 1" nails), with glue.

steps.title: Build, wire, and hang the doors
steps.body: Build each door frame to fit its own opening, 1/4" less in height and width. Staple chicken wire or wire mesh to the inside of each frame, then hang with surface mount hinges. Add a clasp and knob to each door.
materials: Chicken wire or wire mesh and staples

images.alt: A wood cupboard with three chicken-wire-front doors for produce storage
